## Quality Dimensions Reference

The checklist items should be organized across these 9 quality dimensions:

### 1. Completeness (اكتمال)
All required elements are present with no gaps.

- [ ] CHK-001 All mandatory sections are filled
- [ ] CHK-002 No placeholder tokens (`[ALL_CAPS]`) remain without justification
- [ ] CHK-003 All referenced documents exist and are accessible

**Example items:**
> - `CHK-xxx All FRs have at least one AC linked`
> - `CHK-xxx All risk entries have a mitigation plan`

---

### 2. Clarity (وضوح)
Content is unambiguous and understandable to all stakeholders.

- [ ] CHK-004 No banned vague words (fast, easy, secure, flexible, robust, intuitive)
- [ ] CHK-005 All requirements use declarative language (MUST/SHOULD + rationale)
- [ ] CHK-006 Technical jargon is defined or avoided in stakeholder-facing sections

**Example items:**
> - `CHK-xxx Problem statement is specific and not a feature wish`
> - `CHK-xxx Each FR describes ONE clear behavior`

---

### 3. Consistency (اتساق)
No contradictions between sections or documents.

- [ ] CHK-007 ID prefixes follow the unified system (RQ-, FR-, NFR-, BR-, etc.)
- [ ] CHK-008 Dates are in ISO format (YYYY-MM-DD)
- [ ] CHK-009 Terminology is consistent across all sections

**Example items:**
> - `CHK-xxx Scope boundaries in Section 7 match constraints in Section 21`
> - `CHK-xxx NFR targets align with architecture decisions in Section 17`

---

### 4. Measurability (قابلية القياس)
All success criteria have numeric or binary targets.

- [ ] CHK-010 All KPIs have numeric targets with units
- [ ] CHK-011 NFR categories include measurable thresholds (e.g., ≤200ms, 99.9%)
- [ ] CHK-012 Acceptance criteria are verifiable (pass/fail deterministic)

**Example items:**
> - `CHK-xxx Each objective uses SMART format`
> - `CHK-xxx Performance NFRs specify p95/p99 percentiles`

---

### 5. Coverage (تغطية)
All features, users, and scenarios are addressed.

- [ ] CHK-013 All 5 NFR categories addressed (Performance, Scalability, Availability, Security, Compliance)
- [ ] CHK-014 All identified user types have at least one use case scenario
- [ ] CHK-015 Both in-scope and out-of-scope are documented

**Example items:**
> - `CHK-xxx Level 2 (Users) covers primary and secondary user types`
> - `CHK-xxx Integration failure modes are documented`

---

### 6. Edge Cases (الحالات الحدية)
Boundary conditions and negative scenarios are captured.

- [ ] CHK-016 Empty/null input scenarios are addressed
- [ ] CHK-017 Concurrent access or race conditions are considered
- [ ] CHK-018 Network/service failure scenarios have fallback plans

**Example items:**
> - `CHK-xxx Boundary values for all numeric constraints are tested`
> - `CHK-xxx Authentication edge cases (expired token, MFA failure) are documented`

---

### 7. Traceability (قابلية التتبع)
Every element links back to a business need.

- [ ] CHK-019 Every FR traces to at least one RQ or OBJ
- [ ] CHK-020 Every AC links to at least one FR
- [ ] CHK-021 Every critical/high RK has a mitigation plan
- [ ] CHK-022 Traceability matrix (Section 26) has no orphaned references

**Example items:**
> - `CHK-xxx No FR exists without a business justification`
> - `CHK-xxx All ADR entries reference the alternatives considered`

---

### 8. Testability (قابلية الاختبار)
Every requirement can be verified by a test.

- [ ] CHK-023 All FRs use trigger/input/output format
- [ ] CHK-024 All ACs are binary (pass/fail) not subjective
- [ ] CHK-025 Test cases (TC-XXX) cover all critical paths

**Example items:**
> - `CHK-xxx Each FR has a corresponding TC or AC`
> - `CHK-xxx Security requirements reference specific test methods`

---

### 9. Gap Detection (كشف الفجوات)
Missing elements, inconsistencies, or risks are identified.

- [ ] CHK-026 No [NEEDS CLARIFICATION] markers remain (or max 3 critical ones documented)
- [ ] CHK-027 All TODO items have an owner and target resolution date
- [ ] CHK-028 Integration dependencies are identified and risk-assessed

**Example items:**
> - `CHK-xxx All open questions from /syskit.clarify are resolved`
> - `CHK-xxx External service dependencies have SLA commitments documented`

---

## Classification Structure

When generating checklists with `/syskit.checklist`, organize items using these category prefixes:

| Category | Prefix | Usage |
|----------|--------|-------|
| Requirements | `CHK-REQ-` | Functional/non-functional requirements review |
| Architecture | `CHK-ARC-` | Design decisions and technical constraints |
| Security | `CHK-SEC-` | Security, privacy, and compliance items |
| Testing | `CHK-TST-` | Test coverage and acceptance criteria |
| Operations | `CHK-OPS-` | Deployment, monitoring, and support readiness |
| Documentation | `CHK-DOC-` | Documentation completeness |
| Data | `CHK-DAT-` | Data model, governance, and retention |
| Integration | `CHK-INT-` | External service and API readiness |
| UX | `CHK-UX-` | User experience and accessibility |

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Add comments or findings inline using `<!-- comment -->`
- Link to relevant resources or documentation
- Items are numbered sequentially for easy reference
- Failed items should reference the specific section that needs updating
